claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age of your child, and if you have multiple children, please tell me each of their ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that you're looking into Child Benefit for your three children.

Since Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2) are all **under 16 years old**, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. That's a great start!

Let me now check the next requirement for children under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now, the next question is about whether your children are working:

**Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

Given their ages (3, 8, and 2), I would expect the answer is **no, they are not working** - but I just need you to confirm this to proceed.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work - they're just little ones!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course! That makes perfect sense - they're all still very young. So that's a **yes**, they are all working less than 24 hours per week (i.e., not working at all).

Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means they are not personally claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit themselves.

Given their young ages, I'd expect the answer is **yes, they are not receiving any benefits in their own right** - but please confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, none of them get any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are any of the children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) in an apprenticeship?**

Given their ages (3, 8, and 2), I'm certain they're not in any apprenticeships! But just to confirm - none of them are in an apprenticeship, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - they're far too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Absolutely! With little ones aged 2, 3, and 8, apprenticeships are definitely not on the horizon yet! 😊

Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check the residency requirements. You need to meet **at least one** of the following criteria:

**Do any of these apply to you?**

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You usually live in the United Kingdom
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Do you normally live in the UK, or does any of the other options apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you! Living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check that you are responsible for the children. You meet this requirement if **any** of the following apply:

1. **Living with the child** - You live with Alex, Blake, and Charlie
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - You pay at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Do you live with Alex, Blake, and Charlie, or do any of the other options apply?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! Since all three children live with you, you clearly meet the responsibility requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next, I need to check about other potential claimants:

**Is anyone else currently claiming or planning to claim Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

This is important because only one person can receive Child Benefit for each child at a time.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's straightforward then. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check where your children are currently living:

**Are Alex, Blake, and Charlie:**

- **In hospital** - Currently staying in hospital
- **In care** - In local authority care
- **Fostered** - Being fostered
- **Living normally at home** - Living at home with you in normal circumstances

I assume they're all living normally at home with you in Manchester?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - they're all living at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're nearly there! Just one final check:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards accommodation or maintenance for any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?**

In other words, you're not receiving local council payments specifically for their accommodation or upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for them - I'm covering everything myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! That means you've met all the eligibility requirements. Let me navigate to the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! 🎉

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Congratulations! You appear to be ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit!

Based on our assessment, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for all three of your children:

| Child | Age | Eligibility |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| **Alex** | 3 | ✅ Eligible |
| **Blake** | 8 | ✅ Eligible |
| **Charlie** | 2 | ✅ Eligible |

### Summary
All three children qualify because they are under 16, living at home with you in Manchester, you're responsible for them, no one else is claiming for them, and there's no local council involvement in their accommodation or maintenance.

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and each child
3. **Provide birth certificates** (or adoption certificates) for Alex, Blake, and Charlie
4. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### ⚠️ Important Note:
If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be affected by the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you may need to pay some or all of it back through a tax charge.

For more information and to apply, visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) They are not working, (3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) They are not in an apprenticeship, (5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (6) The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (8) Alex is living at home in normal circumstances, and (9) The local council is not paying for their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) They are not working, (3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) They are not in an apprenticeship, (5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (6) The claimant lives with Blake and is responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake is living at home in normal circumstances, and (9) The local council is not paying for their accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) They are not working, (3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) They are not in an apprenticeship, (5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (6) The claimant lives with Charlie and is responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, (8) Charlie is living at home in normal circumstances, and (9) The local council is not paying for their accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears to be eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16, living at home with the claimant in Manchester, UK, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. The claimant meets residency requirements, is responsible for all three children, no other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the local council is not contributing to their accommodation or maintenance.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

Blake (age 8) qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) They are not working, (3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) They are not in an apprenticeship, (5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (6) The claimant lives with Blake and is responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, (8) Blake is living at home in normal circumstances, and (9) The local council is not paying for their accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) qualifies for Child Benefit because: (1) They are under 16 years old, (2) They are not working, (3) They are not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) They are not in an apprenticeship, (5) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (6) The claimant lives with Charlie and is responsible for them, (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, (8) Charlie is living at home in normal circumstances, and (9) The local council is not paying for their accommodation or maintenance.

All children are under 16, living at home with the claimant in Manchester, UK, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. The claimant meets residency requirements, is responsible for all three children, no other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the local council is not contributing to their accommodation or maintenance.
